metric_value: Credit cards used for 90% of online purchases
notes: Key statistic establishing baseline for why alternatives were needed

metric_value: Not well suited for numerous small purchases ($0.05-$5); high processing costs disincentivize merchants
notes: Core problem statement: credit card fees uneconomical for micropayments

metric_value: Failed miserably in U.S.; no viable business case for issuers
notes: Per MasterCard statement; individual smart cards cost $5-$16

metric_value: $5 to $16 depending on microchip memory size
notes: Per MasterCard; infrastructure costs far exceed payback in U.S.

metric_value: Adopted in France/Germany/UK/South Africa/South America due to high credit card fraud and expensive telecom
notes: Strong international adoption vs failed U.S. adoption — Aberdeen correctly identified this bifurcation

metric_name: U.S. smart card adoption — actual outcome
metric_value: EMV chip cards adopted in U.S. from 2015 (liability shift); stored-value smart cards remain niche
notes: U.S. finally adopted chip cards for fraud prevention but stored-value smart cash never materialized

metric_value: ISPs and telcos expected to be particularly proactive; will deploy digital cash into billing infrastructure within next two years
notes: Strong prediction that ISP/telco model would emerge as dominant

metric_value: Mobile carrier billing for digital content (app stores/ring tones) succeeded; ISP billing did not materialize broadly
notes: Aberdeen partially correct — mobile carrier billing succeeded; ISP billing was marginal

metric_name: Bank participation in digital cash market
metric_value: Banks showed typically conservative approach; lack of viable business models prevented bank buy-in
notes: Aberdeen correctly diagnosed banking conservatism as structural inhibitor

metric_name: Bank digital cash participation — actual outcome
metric_value: U.S. banks did not spearhead digital cash; PayPal/Stripe/Square succeeded outside traditional banking
notes: Aberdeen's diagnosis proved accurate — non-bank fintech companies disrupted payments

metric_value: 1 million users claimed by CyberGold
notes: Advertisers pay consumers small amounts for visiting sites/surveys; 1M users claimed

metric_value: Closed August 31 2001; part of wave of digital currency closures (Flooz/Beenz/CyberGold)
notes: Wired reported simultaneous collapse of Flooz/Beenz/CyberGold in August 2001

metric_value: Will challenge traditional means of exchanging value but challenge will take time to unfold; 2-3 year momentum build
notes: Aberdeen's cautious prediction about digital cash timeline

metric_name: Credit card companies long-term digital cash success
metric_value: Greater chance of success for credit card companies with large global installed bases
notes: Aberdeen bases on brand recognition/trust/financial resources/global installed base

metric_value: Visa (NYSE: V) remains dominant; Visa Direct enables real-time payments globally; market cap ~$500B in 2024
notes: Aberdeen's prediction that large credit card companies would win proved highly accurate

metric_value: MasterCard (NYSE: MA) remains dominant global payments network; market cap ~$400B in 2024
notes: Aberdeen's prediction confirmed — large credit card networks won the payments battle

metric_value: Holder of DigiCash patents after DigiCash bankruptcy 1998; digital cash software and service provider
notes: DigiCash went bankrupt 1998; eCash Technologies acquired patents

metric_value: Went bankrupt; acquired by InfoSpace (now Blucora) in 2002
notes: Wikipedia: eCash Technologies acquired by InfoSpace/Blucora in 2002

metric_value: Digital content and commerce enablement for content providers
notes: Succeeded where most digital cash vendors failed

metric_value: Acquired by Amdocs for $275M in 2006; over 6x annual revenues
notes: Finextra/Channel Futures confirmed $275M acquisition in April 2006

metric_value: One of few vendors providing comprehensive digital rights management solutions; ~9 years in business
notes: 9 years old in 1999 means founded approximately 1990; DRM pioneer

metric_value: Acquired by Sony/Philips joint venture for $453M in 2002
notes: Strong outcome for DRM technology; Aberdeen's assessment of InterTrust's strategic position was accurate

metric_value: Hardware-based chip creates market for trusted PCs; ready-made network for security vendors
notes: Wave Systems model; pushes security beyond firewall; government/defense/banking use cases

metric_value: Pay-per-use and rent-to-own models for digital content enabled by trusted hardware
notes: Content via CDs/DVDs/Internet/broadcast media; user-controlled privacy

metric_value: Heavy-wallet approaches not working; lighter versions emerging to maximize user friendliness
notes: CDNow cited as accepting digital wallets; precursor to Apple Pay/Google Wallet

metric_value: Apple Pay/Google Pay/PayPal became dominant; lighter/mobile wallet approach validated
notes: Aberdeen's light-wallet prediction proved correct; heavy-wallet died; mobile wallets succeeded

metric_value: International payment processing across multiple currencies and methods
notes: Most successful long-term outcome of all profiled vendors

metric_value: Acquired by FIS Global for $43B in 2019; one of largest payments industry acquisitions
notes: Extraordinary outcome; Aberdeen profiled WorldPay when it was an early-stage player

metric_value: Spending on the Internet in the U.S. totals in the billions of dollars for 1999
notes: Establishes scale of market; exact figure not given but characterized as 'billions'

metric_name: Digital cash market inhibitor — content quality
metric_value: Digital content must be compelling and tailored to wide geographic online consumer base before digital cash succeeds
notes: Aberdeen correctly identified content quality and branding as prerequisites

metric_value: Underfunded digital cash providers will find scaling difficult
notes: Aberdeen correctly predicted most underfunded vendors would fail

metric_name: Digital cash market growth driver — ISP/telco infrastructure
metric_value: ISPs and telcos have billing infrastructure that eliminates credit card processor friction
notes: Key insight: piggybacking on existing billing relationships
